content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance     •••     Administration     • •     Executive/Legal     • •     Enforcement
(916) 322-5662                    322-5660                  322-5901              322-6441


December 14, 1984


Robert A. Canclini
P.O. Box 1437
Mendocino, CA 95460

Re: A-84-304 5/7

Dear Mr. Canclini:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political
Reform Act has been referred to Kathryn Donovan, an
attorney in the Legal Division of the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact this attorney directly at
(916) 322-5901.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly.
Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex
legal questions, or unless more information is needed to
answer your request, you should expect a response within 21
working days.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
Barbara A. Milman
General Counsel

BAM:plh

December 6, 1984


Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, Ca. 95814


     Sometime in February of 1985 I will announce that I intend to be a candidate for an elective office in Mendocino County (member of the Board of Supervisors). The election for this office will occurr in June of 1986.
     At the present time I am hosting a Talk Program on a local radio station, KPMO, located in Mendocino, for one hour each day, five days a week. The program content involves the discussion of anything a listener may want to talk about and this is accomplished by calling the Radio Station by telephone. Discussion can be on people, news events, weather, history, gardening, government (local, state, and national), etc. I attempt to answer questions and sometimes state my opinion on various subjects.
     My questions to the Commission are in regards to laws about "Equal Time" and are as follows:

     1. How is the program affected by these provisions?
     2. Once I announce, would other candidates or the incumbent supervisor have to be given equal time? The present incumbent Supervisor has a radio program on the same station, one day a week for 45 minutes. This program has been on the air for several years.
     3. Does the subject being discussed trigger the Equal Time Provision? County Government is discussed maybe one hour a week.
     4. Would I have to restrict my discussion by not rendering an opinion regarding County Government?
          Would this satisfy the "Equal Time" provisions?

Any help you can give, and your prompt reply is appreciated.
Also please send me copies of the following:

"Political Reform Act of 1974"
"A Guide for Candidates and Treasurers"
and written material regarding "Equal Time".


Sincerely,

Robert A. Canclini
Robert A. Canclini
P.O. Box 1437
Mendocino,
Calif. 95460
707 937-5283
